known_gaps: External guideline artifacts are not cached or license-verified.
Clinical and safety review is incomplete.
Simulation set is below release coverage requirements.

history.venous_thromboembolism.wording: Have you ever had a blood clot in a leg or lung?

patient.smoking.status.wording: Do you currently smoke, did you smoke in the past, or have you never smoked?

risk.recent_immobility_or_surgery.wording: Have you had surgery recently or been unable to move around for several days?

symptom.ankle_swelling.wording: Have your ankles or lower legs become swollen?

symptom.chest_pain.wording: Do you have chest pain, pressure, tightness, or heaviness?

symptom.confusion.wording: Have you or someone with you noticed new confusion or unusual drowsiness?

symptom.cough.wording: Do you also have a cough?

symptom.cyanosis.wording: Have your lips or skin looked unusually pale, blue, or grey?

symptom.duration.wording: How long have you been short of breath?

symptom.dyspnea.wording: How difficult is it to breathe right now, and can you speak in full sentences?

symptom.dyspnea_at_rest.wording: Are you short of breath even while resting?

symptom.dyspnea_onset.wording: Did the breathing difficulty start suddenly or gradually?

symptom.exertional_dyspnea.wording: Does activity such as walking or climbing stairs bring it on or make it worse?

symptom.fever.wording: Have you had fever or felt feverish?

symptom.hemoptysis.wording: Have you coughed up any blood?

symptom.orthopnea.wording: Is your breathing worse when lying flat, or do you need extra pillows to breathe comfortably?

symptom.palpitations.wording: Have you noticed your heart racing, skipping, or fluttering?

symptom.paroxysmal_nocturnal_dyspnea.wording: Do you wake from sleep short of breath or gasping?

symptom.pleuritic_chest_pain.wording: Is any chest pain sharp or worse when you breathe in?

symptom.sputum.wording: Are you bringing up sputum or phlegm?

symptom.stridor.wording: Is there a harsh or noisy sound when you breathe in, or a choking sensation?

symptom.syncope.wording: Have you fainted, nearly fainted, or felt you might pass out?

symptom.unilateral_leg_pain_swelling.wording: Do you have new pain or swelling in one leg?

symptom.wheeze.wording: Have you noticed wheezing or a whistling sound when breathing?

guideline_behavior.rationale: Interview behavior derived from guidelines may change with recommendations.

local_jurisdiction.rationale: Local pathways and administrative rules may change quickly.

safety_critical.rationale: Safety behavior and escalation knowledge require frequent monitoring.

stable_semantic.rationale: Atomic patient-reportable concepts are relatively stable but still require periodic review.

terminology_mapping.rationale: Terminology and interoperability baselines have independent releases.

clinical_guideline.rationale: Daily automated monitoring provides early detection for high-impact guideline changes; full review occurs only when change is detected or the review date is reached.

nice_guidance.rationale: NICE guidance is versioned and may be amended; weekly monitoring detects changes without implying weekly clinical re-review.

public_health_guidance.rationale: Public-health pages can change between formal releases; weekly monitoring is appropriate for the research package.

terminology_server.rationale: Terminology servers publish versioned content; monthly baseline monitoring balances stability and acquisition cost.

assertions: Shortness of breath, chest pain, and coughing up blood are features that require assessment when pulmonary embolism is considered.
One-sided leg swelling or pain, recent immobility or surgery, and previous venous thromboembolism are relevant history features.
Formal probability tools require clinical assessment and are not executed by this interview package.
artifacts.title: Venous thromboembolic diseases: diagnosis, management and thrombophilia testing

assertions: Exertional breathlessness, cough, sputum, wheeze, smoking history, night waking, ankle swelling, chest pain, and haemoptysis are relevant history features.
Severe breathlessness, cyanosis, impaired consciousness, and worsening peripheral oedema affect the need for hospital-level assessment.
These features support interview coverage and do not establish a diagnosis.
artifacts.title: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management

assertions: Severe breathing difficulty, inability to speak normally, colour change, and sudden confusion are emergency warning features.
Coughing up blood, one-sided leg pain or swelling, and palpitations warrant urgent assessment.
Breathlessness worse with normal activity or lying down and associated ankle swelling warrant medical review.
artifacts.title: Shortness of breath

assertions: Sudden blue or grey colour change with breathing difficulty is an emergency warning feature.
artifacts.title: Blue or grey skin or lips (cyanosis)
